summary: Arqel dogfood loop — Round 19: re-detect against main (89 framework bugs fixed across rounds 0-18). Deepest probes into the least-covered surfaces (audit attribute filtering, realtime broadcast dispatch, mcp tools, cli generators, export xlsx/formatting, i18n/locale, theme, command palette, a11y, edge-case interactions between traits). Seen-filtered (95 sigs), adversarial verify. Round 17 was CLEAN but Round 18 found 1 (export DateColumn mode/format ignored, MED) -> consecutive-clean counter RESET to 0/2. Detection curve deep in the tail (...4,1,0,1); remaining bugs are rare/low-severity/isolated. A clean round is the expected outcome; report ONLY a concrete, live, reproduced defect at a genuinely distinct entry point. Stop after 2 consecutive clean rounds.
logs: Round 19 detection: 1 from clusters = 1 after dedup/seen-filter
Round 19 verify: 1 confirmed NEW framework bugs, 0 app-misuse, 0 not-a-bug

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Repeater/Builder nested sub-field schema serialized to only {name,type}: options, label, placeholder dropped -> nested SelectField renders an empty dropdown
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Route nested schema fields through Arqel\Core\Support\FieldSchemaSerializer instead of the dead `method_exists($f,'toArray')` branch at both RepeaterField::getTypeSpecificProps() and Block::toArray(); align the emitted shape with what RepeaterInput.tsx/BuilderInput.tsx actually read (flat `label`/`placeholder`/`options`, i.e. surface getTypeSpecificProps() onto the sub-field object), since the canonical serializer nests options under `props`.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced directly against current main. Confirmed no Field subclass defines toArray() (grep over packages/fields/** and fields-advanced/** finds only Block::toArray and Step::toArray; base Arqel\Fields\Field at packages/fields/src/Field.php has none). Therefore `method_exists($child,'toArray')` at RepeaterField.php:174 and Block.php:72 is ALWAYS false, and both fall back to the {name,type}-only branch. Canonical field serialization is FieldSchemaSerializer::serializeOne() (packages/core/src/Support/FieldSchemaSerializer.php:73) which emits label/placeholder/props(options) but is never invoked for nested fields. Live PHP repro via showcase autoloader: RepeaterField::make('items')->schema([TextField('title')->label('My Title')->placeholder('Type here'), SelectField('status')->options([a=>Active,b=>Banned])])->getTypeSpecificProps()['schema'] == [{name:title,type:text},{name:status,type:select}] — label/placeholder/options all dropped. Identical repro for an anonymous Block subclass via Block::toArray(). Consumer impact verified: RepeaterInput.tsx is shipped and registered (register.ts:34-35) and reads sub.label (subLabel line 572), field.placeholder (248/278/326) and builds nested <select> options from field.options (284-307); BuilderInput reads field.placeholder/field.options identically. So nested SelectField renders an empty/unselectable dropdown and nested labels fall back to the raw field name. Test masks it: RepeaterInput.test.tsx 'renders a select sub-field with the schema-supplied options' hand-builds schema with flat options, never feeding a real PHP-serialized field. This is a framework bug (defective framework serialization, not app misuse), same documented-but-unwired family as #204/#205/#206 but at two distinct, previously-unswept entry points. Severity high: nested selects are functionally unusable. Caveat for the fixer: RepeaterInput/BuilderInput read FLAT options/label/placeholder, while serializeOne nests options under `props` — a naive serializer route-through would still leave field.options empty; the fix must reconcile the PHP output shape with the flat reads (or update the TSX).
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence:
RepeaterField::getTypeSpecificProps() (packages/fields-advanced/src/Types/RepeaterField.php:172-186) serializes each nested schema Field via `method_exists($child,'toArray') ? $child->toArray() : ['name'=>..., 'type'=>...]`. Block::toArray() (packages/fields-advanced/src/Blocks/Block.php:64-90, used by BuilderField::getTypeSpecificProps() line 185) does the IDENTICAL thing for each inner-form Field. But no Arqel\Fields\Field (nor any field type) defines a toArray() method — canonical field serialization lives in Arqel\Core\Support\FieldSchemaSerializer::serializeOne(), which is NEVER invoked for nested fields. So `method_exists($field,'toArray')` is ALWAYS false and every nested field collapses to {name,type} only.

Live repro (showcase, real RepeaterField): `RepeaterField::make('items')->schema([(new TextField('title'))->label('My Title')->placeholder('Type here'), (new SelectField('status'))->options(['a'=>'Active','b'=>'Banned'])])` -> getTypeSpecificProps()['schema'] == [{"name":"title","type":"text"},{"name":"status","type":"select"}]. Expected: label/placeholder for the text field and options={a:Active,b:Banned} for the select.

Consumer impact: RepeaterInput.tsx (packages-js/fields-advanced/src/repeater/RepeaterInput.tsx) IS shipped and registered (register.ts:34-35) and reads sub.label (line 76), sub.placeholder (78), and sub.options to build the nested <select> options (lines 283-307) -> nested SelectField renders ONLY the '—' placeholder (options never arrive). BuilderInput.tsx reads field.placeholder (276,306) and field.options (313) identically. So nested selects in a repeater/builder are unselectable, nested labels fall back to the raw field name, and placeholders are lost.

This is the SAME documented-but-unwired serialization family as #205 (Action::toArray formFields), #204 (SelectField options), #206 (Column state) — but at two DISTINCT, previously-unswept entry points (nested repeater schema + builder block schema). Tests mask it: RepeaterInput.test.tsx:346-357 hand-builds a schema WITH options, and there is no PHP integration test feeding a real nested SelectField through serialization. Fix: route nested fields through FieldSchemaSerializer (or add a real Field::toArray) at both RepeaterField.php:172 and Block.php:64.
